name: ArraySized

aliases.name: ArraySized
aliases.comment:
An `Array` where you know more about the amount of elements.


## result type

    -- amount >= 5
    : ArraySized (Min N5) ...

    -- 2 <= amount <= 12
    : ArraySized (In N2 (Add12 a_)) ...


### argument type

    -- = 15
    : ArraySized (Exactly N15) ...

    -- amount >= 4
    : ArraySized (In (Add4 minMinus4_) max_) ...

    -- 4 <= amount <= 15
    : ArraySized (In (Add4 minMinus4_) N15) ...


## stored type

in your `Model` for example (which means: no type variables)

    -- = 15
    : ArraySized (Exactly N15) ...

    -- amount >= 4
    : ArraySized (Min N4) ...

    -- 4 <= amount <= 15
    : ArraySized (In N4 N15) ...

`==` crashes elm. Try [other comparison methods](#length-compare)

aliases.name: In

values.name: areAll
values.comment:
Whether all elements satisfy a test.

    ArraySized.l2 2 3 |> ArraySized.areAll (\n -> n <= 4)
    --> True

    ArraySized.l2 2 7 |> ArraySized.areAll (\n -> n <= 4)
    --> False

    ArraySized.empty |> ArraySized.areAll (\n -> n <= 4)
    --> True
values.type: (element -> Basics.Bool) -> ArraySized.ArraySized length_ element -> Basics.Bool

values.name: areAllFilled
values.comment:
If every `Emptiable` is `filled`, all of the values.
If any element is `empty`, `empty`

    import Emptiable exposing (filled, fillMap)

    ArraySized.empty
        |> ArraySized.areAllFilled
        |> fillMap ArraySized.toList
    --> filled []

    ArraySized.l3 (filled 1) (filled 2) (filled 3)
        |> ArraySized.areAllFilled
        |> fillMap ArraySized.toList
    --> filled [ 1, 2, 3 ]

    ArraySized.l3 (filled 1) Emptiable.empty (filled 3)
        |> ArraySized.areAllFilled
    --> Emptiable.empty

Funnily, this can sometimes even be nicer than `mapN`/`andMap`:

    groupCall =
        ArraySized.l5 aUser bUser cUser dUser eUser
            |> ArraySized.map .phoneNumber
            |> ArraySized.areAllFilled

    -- vs
    groupCall =
        map5 Toop.T5
            aUser.phoneNumber
            bUser.phoneNumber
            cUser.phoneNumber
            dUser.phoneNumber
            eUser.phoneNumber
values.type: ArraySized.ArraySized length (Emptiable.Emptiable value possiblyOrNever) -> Emptiable.Emptiable (ArraySized.ArraySized length value) possiblyOrNever

values.name: drop
values.comment:
Elements after a certain number of elements in a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/).

    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))
    import N exposing (n2)

    ArraySized.l4 0 1 2 3
        |> ArraySized.drop Down n2
        --: ArraySized (In N2 (Add2 a_)) number_
        |> ArraySized.toList
    --> [ 0, 1 ]

    between6And10Elements
        |> ArraySized.drop Up n2
    --: ArraySized (In N4 (Add10 a_)) ...
values.type: Linear.DirectionLinear -> N.N (N.In dropped_ droppedAtLeast_ (N.Is (N.Diff minTaken N.To min) (N.Diff maxTaken N.To max))) -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In min max) element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In minTaken maxTaken) element

values.name: element
values.comment:
The element at a valid position in a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/).

    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))
    import N exposing (n1)

    ArraySized.l4 0 1 2 3
        |> ArraySized.element ( Up, n1 )
    --> 1

    ArraySized.l4 0 1 2 3
        |> ArraySized.element ( Down, n1 )
    --> 2
values.type: ( Linear.DirectionLinear, N.N (N.In indexMin_ minLengthMinus1 indexDifference_) ) -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In (N.Add1 minLengthMinus1) maxLength_) element -> element

values.name: elementAlter
values.comment:
Change the element at an index in a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/) based on its previous value.

    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))
    import N exposing (n0)

    ArraySized.l3 1 20 30
        |> ArraySized.elementAlter ( Up, n0 ) (\x -> x * 10)
        --: ArraySized (In n3 (Add3 a)) number_
        |> ArraySized.toList
    --> [ 10, 20, 30 ]

    ArraySized.l3 1 20 30
        |> ArraySized.elementAlter ( Down, n0 ) (\x -> -x)
        --: ArraySized (In n3 (Add3 a)) number_
        |> ArraySized.toList
    --> [ 1, 20, -30 ]

An index that's out of bounds is ignored.

    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))
    import N exposing (n3)

    ArraySized.l3 1 20 30
        |> ArraySized.elementAlter ( Up, n3 ) (\x -> x * 10)
        --: ArraySized (In n3 (Add3 a)) number_
        |> ArraySized.toList
    --> [ 1, 20, 30 ]
values.type: ( Linear.DirectionLinear, N.N index_ ) -> (element -> element) -> ArraySized.ArraySized length element -> ArraySized.ArraySized length element

values.name: elementRemove
values.comment:
Kick an element out of the `ArraySized`
at a given index in a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/)

    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))
    import N exposing (n5)

    removeLast between1And10Elements =
        between1And10Elements
            |> ArraySized.elementRemove ( Down, n0 )
values.type: ( Linear.DirectionLinear, N.N (N.In indexMin_ minLengthMinus1 indexDifference_) ) -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In (N.Add1 minLengthMinus1) (N.Add1 maxLengthMinus1)) element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In minLengthMinus1 maxLengthMinus1) element

values.name: elementReplace
values.comment:
Set the element at an index in a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/).

    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))
    import N exposing (n1, n2)

    ArraySized.l3 "I" "am" "ok"
        |> ArraySized.elementReplace ( Up, n2 )
            (\() -> "confusion")
        --: ArraySized (In n3 (Add3 a)) String
        |> ArraySized.toList
    --> [ "I", "am", "confusion" ]

    ArraySized.l3 "I" "am" "ok"
        |> ArraySized.elementReplace ( Down, n1 )
            (\() -> "feel")
        --: ArraySized (In n3 (Add3 a)) String
        |> ArraySized.toList
    --> [ "I", "feel", "ok" ]

An index that's out of bounds is ignored
and the element replacement is never evaluated.

    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))
    import N exposing (n3)

    ArraySized.l3 "I" "am" "ok"
        |> ArraySized.elementReplace ( Down, n3 )
            (\() -> "feel")
        --: ArraySized (In n3 (Add3 a)) String
        |> ArraySized.toList
    --> [ "I", "am", "ok" ]
values.type: ( Linear.DirectionLinear, N.N index_ ) -> (() -> element) -> ArraySized.ArraySized length element -> ArraySized.ArraySized length element

values.name: empty
values.comment:
No elements

    ArraySized.empty
    --: ArraySized (In N0 atLeast0_) element
        |> ArraySized.push ":)"
    --: ArraySized (In N1 (Add1 atLeast0_)) String
values.type: ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In N.N0 atLeast0_) element_

values.name: fills
values.comment:
Take every `filled value`, drop every `empty`.

    import N exposing (n0)
    import Emptiable exposing (filled)

    ArraySized.l3 (filled "This") Emptiable.empty (filled "fine")
        |> ArraySized.fills
        --: ArraySized (In N0 (Add3 a_)) String
        |> ArraySized.toList
    --> [ "This", "fine" ]

[`map |> fills` to get the same functionality as "filterMap"](https://github.com/lue-bird/elm-typesafe-array/blob/master/Q%20%26%20A.md#no-filtermap-only-fills).

    import N exposing (n0)

    ArraySized.l3 "1.2" "2" "hello"
        |> ArraySized.map (String.toInt >> Emptiable.fromMaybe)
        |> ArraySized.fills
        --: ArraySized (In N0 (Add3 a_)) Int
        |> ArraySized.toList
    --> [ 2 ]
values.type: ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In min_ max) (Emptiable.Emptiable value possiblyOrNever_) -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In N.N0 max) value

values.name: fold
values.comment:
A fold in a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/)
where the initial result is the first element in the [`ArraySized`](#ArraySized).

    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))

    ArraySized.l3 234 345 543
        |> ArraySized.fold Up max
    --> 543

    ArraySized.l3 "go" "to" "uni"
        |> ArraySized.fold Down
            (\word soFar -> soFar ++ " " ++ word)
    --> "uni to go"
values.type: Linear.DirectionLinear -> (element -> element -> element) -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In (N.Add1 minLengthMinus1_) maxLength_) element -> element

values.name: foldFrom
values.comment:
Reduce an `ArraySized` in a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/indique/elm-linear-direction/latest/).

    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))

    ArraySized.l4 'l' 'i' 'v' 'e'
        |> ArraySized.foldFrom "" Down String.cons
    --> "live"

    ArraySized.l4 'l' 'i' 'v' 'e'
        |> ArraySized.foldFrom "" Up String.cons
    --> "evil"

    sum =
        ArraySized.foldFrom 0 Up (\soFar n -> soFar + n)

    product =
        ArraySized.foldFrom 0 Up (\soFar n -> soFar * n)
values.type: result -> Linear.DirectionLinear -> (element -> result -> result) -> ArraySized.ArraySized length_ element -> result

values.name: fromArray
values.comment:
Create from an `Array`.
As every `Array` has `>= 0` elements:

    arrayFromSomeOtherLibrary |> ArraySized.fromArray
    --: ArraySized (Min N0)

Don't use it for construction:

    ArraySized.fromArray
        (Array.fromList [ 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 ])
    -- big no

Make sure the compiler knows as much as you about the amount of elements!

    ArraySized.l7 0 1 2 3 4 5 6 -- ok

    ArraySized.until n6 -- big yes

["wrap early, unwrap late"](https://elm-radio.com/episode/wrap-early-unwrap-late)
values.type: Array.Array element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (N.Min N.N0) element

values.name: fromEmptiable
values.comment:
On `Just` [`ArraySized.l1`](#l1), on `Nothing` [`empty`](#empty).

    import N exposing (n0)
    import Emptiable exposing (filled)

    filled "hi"
        |> ArraySized.fromEmptiable
        --: ArraySized (In N0 (Add1 a_))
        |> ArraySized.toList
    --> [ "hi" ]

    Emptiable.empty
        |> ArraySized.fromEmptiable
        --: ArraySized (In N0 (Add1 a_))
        |> ArraySized.toList
    --> []

Sadly, they way natural number constraints are defined,
`possiblyOrNever` can't be transferred.
values.type: Emptiable.Emptiable element possiblyOrNever_ -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In N.N0 (N.Add1 a_)) element

values.name: fromList
values.comment:
Create from a `List`.
As every `List` has `>= 0` elements:

    listFromSomeOtherLibrary |> ArraySized.fromList
    --: ArraySized (Min N0)

Don't use for construction:

    ArraySized.fromList [ 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 ]
    -- big no!

Make sure the compiler knows as much as you about the amount of elements!

    ArraySized.l7 0 1 2 3 4 5 6 -- ok

    ArraySized.up n7 -- big yes

["wrap early, unwrap late"](https://elm-radio.com/episode/wrap-early-unwrap-late)
values.type: List.List element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (N.Min N.N0) element

values.name: glue
values.comment:
Attach elements of an `ArraySized` with an exact amount of elements to a given [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/).

    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))
    import N exposing (n3)

    ArraySized.l3 1 2 3
        |> ArraySized.glue Up n3 (ArraySized.l3 4 5 6)
        --: ArraySized (In n6 (Add6 a_)) number_
        |> ArraySized.toList
    --> [ 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 ]

    ArraySized.l3 1 2 3
        |> ArraySized.glue Down n3 (ArraySized.l3 4 5 6)
        --: ArraySized (In n6 (Add6 a_)) number_
        |> ArraySized.toList
    --> [ 4, 5, 6, 1, 2, 3 ]
values.type: Linear.DirectionLinear -> N.N (N.In added atLeastAdded_ (N.Is (N.Diff min N.To minLengthSum) (N.Diff max N.To minSumMax))) -> ArraySized.ArraySized (N.Exactly added) element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In min max) element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In minLengthSum minSumMax) element

values.name: glueAtLeast
values.comment:
Attach elements of an `ArraySized` to a given [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/).

    ArraySized.l3 1 2 3
        |> ArraySized.glueAtLeast Up n3 atLeast3Elements
    --: ArraySized (Min N6) ...

    ArraySized.l3 1 2 3
        |> ArraySized.glueAtLeast Down n3 atLeast3Elements
    --: ArraySized (Min N6) ...
values.type: Linear.DirectionLinear -> N.N (N.In minAdded atLeastMinAdded_ (N.Is (N.Diff min N.To minLengthSum) is_)) -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In minAdded maxAdded_) element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In min maxLength_) element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (N.Min minLengthSum) element

values.name: glueIn
values.comment:
Attach elements of an `ArraySized` which has multiple possible amounts to a given [direction](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/).

    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))
    import N exposing (n3, n5)

    ArraySized.l3 1 2 3
        |> ArraySized.glueIn Up
            ( n3, n5 )
            between3And5Elements
    --: ArraySized (In N6 (Add8 a))

    ArraySized.l3 1 2 3
        |> ArraySized.glueIn Down
            ( n3, n5 )
            between3And5Elements
    --: ArraySized (In N6 (Add8 a))

Use [`glue`](#glue) to glue an `Exact` amount of elements.
values.type: Linear.DirectionLinear -> ( N.N (N.In addedMin atLeastAddedMin_ (N.Is (N.Diff min N.To minLengthSum) addedMinDiff1_)), N.N (N.In addedMax atLeastAddedMax_ (N.Is addedMaxDiff0_ (N.Diff max N.To maxLengthSum))) ) -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In addedMin addedMax) element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In min max) element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In minLengthSum maxLengthSum) element

values.name: has
values.comment:
Compare its length to a given exact length. Does it match or is it `BelowOrAbove`?

    import N exposing (n7)

    chooseFormation :
        ArraySized (In min N50) Character
        -> Formation
    chooseFormation characters =
        case characters |> ArraySized.has n7 of
            Ok exactly7 ->
                SpecialAttack exactly7

            Err (N.Below l6AtLeast) ->
                Retreat l6AtLeast

            Err (N.Above l8AtLeast) ->
                Fight l8AtLeast
values.type: N.N (N.In comparedAgainstMin (N.Add1 comparedAgainstMaxMinus1) comparedAgainstDifference) -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In min max) element -> Result.Result (N.BelowOrAbove (ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In min comparedAgainstMaxMinus1) element) (ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In (N.Add1 comparedAgainstMin) max) element)) (ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In comparedAgainstMin (N.Add1 comparedAgainstMaxMinus1)) element)

values.name: hasAtLeast
values.comment:
Is its length below (`Err`) or atLeast (`Ok`) as big as a given `N`?

    import N exposing (n5)

    first5 :
        ArraySized (In minLength_ max) element
        -> Maybe (ArraySized (In N5 max) element)
    first5 arraySized =
        case arraySized |> ArraySized.hasAtLeast n5 of
            N.Below _ ->
                Nothing

            N.EqualOrGreater atLeast5 ->
                Just atLeast5
values.type: N.N (N.In lowerLimitMin (N.Add1 lowerLimitMaxMinus1) lowerLimitDifference_) -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In min max) element -> Result.Result (ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In min lowerLimitMaxMinus1) element) (ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In lowerLimitMin max) element)

values.name: hasAtMost
values.comment:
Is its length atMost (`Ok`) or above (`Err`) a given length?

    -- at least 3 and only up to 50 tags
    tag :
        ArraySized (In (Add3 orHigherMin_) N50) String
        -> a
        -> Tagged a

    tagIfValidTags :
        ArraySized (In (Add3 orHigherMin_) max)
        -> a
        -> Maybe (Tagged a)
    tagIfValidTags array value =
        case
            array
                |> ArraySized.fromArray
                |> ArraySized.hasAtMost n50
        of
            Ok atMost53 ->
                tag value atMost53 |> Just

            Err _ ->
                Nothing
values.type: N.N (N.In upperLimitMin upperLimitMax upperLimitDifference_) -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In min max) element -> Result.Result (ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In (N.Add1 upperLimitMin) max) element) (ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In min upperLimitMax) element)

values.name: hasIn
values.comment:
Compared to a range from a lower to an upper bound, is its length in, `BelowOrAbove` range?

    import N exposing (n10, n16)

    chooseFormation :
        ArraySized (In minLength_ N50) Character
        -> Formation
    chooseFormation characters =
        case characters |> ArraySized.hasIn ( n10, n16 ) of
            Ok between10And16 ->
                SpecialAttack between10And16

            Err (N.Below n9AtMost) ->
                Retreat n9AtMost

            Err (N.Above n17AtLeast) ->
                Fight n17AtLeast
values.type: ( N.N (N.In lowerLimitMin (N.Add1 lowerLimitMaxMinus1) lowerLimitDifference_), N.N (N.In upperLimitMin upperLimitMax upperLimitDifference_) ) -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In min max) element -> Result.Result (N.BelowOrAbove (ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In min lowerLimitMaxMinus1) element) (ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In (N.Add1 upperLimitMin) max) element)) (ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In lowerLimitMin upperLimitMax) element)

values.name: insert
values.comment:
Put an element in the `ArraySized` at a given index
in a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/)

    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))
    import N exposing (n1, n2)

    ArraySized.l3 'a' 'c' 'd'
        |> ArraySized.insert ( Up, n1 ) 'b'
        --: ArraySized (In N4 (Add4 a_)) Char
        |> ArraySized.toList
    --> [ 'a', 'b', 'c', 'd' ]

    ArraySized.l3 'a' 'c' 'd'
        |> ArraySized.insert ( Down, n2 ) 'b'
        --: ArraySized (In N4 (Add4 a_)) Char
        |> ArraySized.toList
    --> [ 'a', 'b', 'c', 'd' ]
values.type: ( Linear.DirectionLinear, N.N (N.In indexMin_ min indexDifference_) ) -> element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In min max) element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In (N.Add1 min) (N.Add1 max)) element

values.name: intersperseAtLeast
values.comment:
Place a value between all members.

To get the correct final length type, we need to give the current minimum length as an arguments.

    import N exposing (n3)

    atLeast3Turtles
        |> ArraySized.intersperseAtLeast n3 "on"
    --→ "turtles" "on" "turtles" "on" "turtles" ...
    --: ArraySized (Min N5) String
values.type: N.N (N.In min minAtLeast_ (N.Is (N.Diff min N.To (N.Add1 minDoubleMinus1)) minDiff1_)) -> element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In min maxLength_) element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (N.Min minDoubleMinus1) element

values.name: intersperseIn
values.comment:
Place a value between all members.

To get the correct final length type,
we need to give the current `(` minimum `,` maximum `)` length as an arguments.

    import N exposing (n3)

    ArraySized.l3 "turtles" "turtles" "turtles"
        |> ArraySized.intersperseIn ( n3, n3 ) "on"
        --: ArraySized (In N5 (Add5 a_)) String
        |> ArraySized.toList
    --> [ "turtles", "on", "turtles", "on", "turtles" ]
values.type: ( N.N (N.In min atLeastMinLength_ (N.Is (N.Diff min N.To (N.Add1 minDoubleLengthMinus1)) minDiff1_)), N.N (N.In max atLeastMaxLength_ (N.Is maxDiff0_ (N.Diff max N.To (N.Add1 maxDoubleLengthMinus1)))) ) -> element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In min max) element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In minDoubleLengthMinus1 maxDoubleLengthMinus1) element

values.name: isAny
values.comment:
Whether any elements satisfy a test.

    ArraySized.l2 300 -5 |> ArraySized.isAny (\n -> n <= 4)
    --> True

    ArraySized.l2 5 5 |> ArraySized.isAny (\n -> n <= 4)
    --> False

    ArraySized.empty |> ArraySized.isAny (\n -> n <= 4)
    --> False
values.type: (element -> Basics.Bool) -> ArraySized.ArraySized length_ element -> Basics.Bool

values.name: l1
values.comment: Create an `ArraySized` with exactly 1 element
values.type: element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In N.N1 (N.Add1 a_)) element

values.name: l10
values.comment: Create an `ArraySized` with exactly 10 elements in this order
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In N.N10 (N.Add10 a_)) element

values.name: l11
values.comment: Create an `ArraySized` with exactly 11 elements in this order
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In N.N11 (N.Add11 a_)) element

values.comment: Create an `ArraySized` with exactly 12 elements in this order
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In N.N12 (N.Add12 a_)) element

values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In N.N13 (N.Add13 a_)) element

values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In N.N14 (N.Add14 a_)) element

values.comment: Create an `ArraySized` with exactly 15 elements in this order
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In N.N15 (N.Add15 a_)) element

values.comment: Create an `ArraySized` with exactly 16 elements in this order
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In N.N16 (N.Add16 a_)) element

values.name: l2
values.comment: Create an `ArraySized` with exactly 2 elements in this order
values.type: element -> element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In N.N2 (N.Add2 a_)) element

values.comment: Create an `ArraySized` with exactly 3 elements in this order

values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In N.N4 (N.Add4 a_)) element

values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In N.N5 (N.Add5 a_)) element

values.name: l6
values.comment: Create an `ArraySized` with exactly 6 elements in this order
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In N.N6 (N.Add6 a_)) element

values.name: l7
values.comment: Create an `ArraySized` with exactly 7 elements in this order
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In N.N7 (N.Add7 a_)) element

values.name: l8
values.comment: Create an `ArraySized` with exactly 8 elements in this order
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In N.N8 (N.Add8 a_)) element

values.name: l9
values.comment: Create an `ArraySized` with exactly 9 elements in this order
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In N.N9 (N.Add9 a_)) element

values.name: length
values.comment:
The amount of elements.

    import N exposing (n3)

    ArraySized.l3 1 2 3
        |> ArraySized.length
        --: N (N.In N3 (Add3 a_))
        |> N.toInt
    --> 3

    between3And5Elements |> ArraySized.length
    --: N (N.In N3 (Add5 a_))

    atLeast3Elements |> ArraySized.length
    --: N (Min N3)
values.type: ArraySized.ArraySized length element_ -> N.N length

values.name: map
values.comment:
Change every element.

    import N exposing (n25)

    aToZ : ArraySized (In N26 (N26Plus a_)) Char
    aToZ =
        ArraySized.until n25
            |> ArraySized.map inABC

    inABC index =
        ('a' |> Char.toCode)
            + (index |> N.toInt)
            |> Char.fromCode

Oh look, more type-safety!
values.type: (element -> mappedElement) -> ArraySized.ArraySized length element -> ArraySized.ArraySized length mappedElement

values.name: maxOpen
values.comment:
Make an `ArraySized` with a fixed maximum length fit into functions with require a higher maximum length.

Designing argument and stored types as broad as possible:

    atMost18Elements : ArraySized (In min_ N18) ...

The argument in `atMost18Elements` should also fit in `atMost19Elements` for example

    atMost19Elements theArgument -- error

    atMost19Elements (theArgument |> ArraySized.maxOpen n18)
values.type: N.N (N.In max newMax difference_) -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In min max) element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In min newMax) element

values.name: maxUp
values.comment:
Have a specific maximum in mind? → [`maxOpen`](#maximum)

Want to increase the upper bound by a fixed amount? ↓

    maxUp4 : ArraySized (In min max) -> ArraySized (In min (Add4 max))
    maxUp4 =
        ArraySized.maxUp n4

When is this useful? Very rarely, to preserve type variables.
More in [`N.maxUp`](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/lue-bird/elm-bounded-nat/latest/N#maxUp)
values.type: N.N (N.In increase_ increaseAtLeast_ (N.Is (N.Diff max N.To maxIncreased) diff1_)) -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In min max) element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In min maxIncreased) element

values.name: minDrop
values.type: Linear.DirectionLinear -> N.N (N.In dropped_ atLeastDropped_ (N.Is (N.Diff minTaken N.To min) is_)) -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In min max) element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In minTaken max) element

values.name: minElementRemove
values.comment:
Kick out the element at an index in a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/).

    removeLast =
        TypeSized.minElementRemove ( Down, n0 )
values.type: ( Linear.DirectionLinear, N.N (N.In indexMin_ minLengthMinus1 indexDifference_) ) -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In (N.Add1 minLengthMinus1) max) element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In minLengthMinus1 max) element

values.name: minInsert
values.comment:
Put a new element at an index in a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/).

    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))
    import N exposing (n0, n1)

    atLeast5Elements
        |> ArraySized.minInsert ( Down, n1 )
            "before last"
    --: ArraySized (Min N6) String

    cons :
        element
        -> ArraySized (In min maxLength_) element
        -> ArraySized (Min (Add1 min)) element
    cons =
        ArraySized.minInsert ( Up, n0 )
values.type: ( Linear.DirectionLinear, N.N (N.In indexMin_ min indexDifference_) ) -> element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In min maxLength_) element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (N.Min (N.Add1 min)) element

values.name: minLower
values.comment:
Use a lower minimum length in the type.

    [ atLeast3Elements
    , atLeast4Elements
    ]

elm complains that

> all the previous elements in the list are `ArraySized (Min N3) ...`

    [ atLeast3Elements
    , atLeast4Elements
        |> ArraySized.minDownLength n3
    ]
values.type: N.N (N.In newMinLength min newMinDifference_) -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In min max) element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In newMinLength max) element

values.name: minPush
values.comment:
Put a new element after the others.

    atLeast5Elements
        |> ArraySized.minPush "becomes the last"
    --: ArraySized (Min N6) String
values.type: element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In min maxLength_) element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (N.Min (N.Add1 min)) element

values.name: noMax
values.comment:
Convert the `ArraySized (In min ...)` to a `ArraySized (Min min)`.

    between4And10Elements |> ArraySized.toMin
    --: ArraySized (Min N4) ...

There is only 1 situation you should use this.

To make these the same type.

    [ atLeast1Element, between1And10Elements ]

Elm complains:

> But all the previous elements in the list are
> `ArraySized (Min N1) ...`

    [ atLeast1Element
    , between1And10Elements |> ArraySized.toMin
    ]
values.type: ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In min max_) element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (N.Min min) element

values.name: push
values.comment:
Equivalent to `insert n0 Down`. Put a new element after all the others.

    between5And10Elements
        |> ArraySized.push "becomes the last"
    --: ArraySized (In N6 (Add11 a_)) String
values.type: element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In min max) element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In (N.Add1 min) (N.Add1 max)) element

values.name: random
values.comment:
`Random.Generator` for a given amount of elements.

    import N exposing (n5)

    ArraySized.random n5 (Random.float 0 1)

    --: Random.Generator (ArraySized (In N5 (Add5 a_)) Float)
    N.random
values.type: N.N (N.In min max difference_) -> Random.Generator element -> Random.Generator (ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In min max) element)

values.name: repeat
values.comment:
An `ArraySized` with a given amount of same elements.

    import N exposing (n4)

    ArraySized.repeat n4 'L'
        --: ArraySized (In N4 (Add4 a)) Char
        |> ArraySized.toList
    --> [ 'L', 'L', 'L', 'L' ]

    ArraySized.repeat atLeast3 'L'
    --: ArraySized (Min N3) Char
values.type: N.N (N.In min max difference_) -> element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In min max) element

values.name: reverse
values.comment:
Alias to `ArraySized.order Down`: flip the order of the elements.

    ArraySized.l4 "l" "i" "v" "e"
        |> ArraySized.reverse
        --: ArraySized (In N4 (Add4 a_)) String
        |> ArraySized.toList
    --> [ "e", "v", "i", "l" ]
values.type: ArraySized.ArraySized length element -> ArraySized.ArraySized length element

values.name: take
values.comment:
A certain number of elements from a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/).

    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))
    import N exposing (n7)

    ArraySized.l8 0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7
        |> ArraySized.take Up n7
        --: ArraySized (In N7 (Add7 a_)) number_
        |> ArraySized.toList
    --> [ 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 ]

Use [`takeAtMost`](#takeAtMost) if you don't know the exact amount of elements to take.
values.type: Linear.DirectionLinear -> N.N (N.In taken atLeastTaken (N.Is (N.Diff minNotTaken_ N.To min) is_)) -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In min maxLength_) element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In taken atLeastTaken) element

values.name: takeAtMost
values.comment:
A certain number of elements from a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/).

    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))
    import N exposing (n7)

    ArraySized.l8 0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7
        |> ArraySized.takeAtMost Up n7 between3And7
    --: ArraySized (In N3 (Add7 a_)) ...

The first number is the maximum taken amount. The second number is the amount of taken elements.

Use [`take`](ArraySized#take) if you know the exact amount of elements to take.
values.type: Linear.DirectionLinear -> N.N (N.In maxTaken atLeastMaxTaken (N.Is (N.Diff minNotTaken_ N.To min) is_)) -> N.N (N.In minTaken maxTaken takenDifference_) -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In min maxLength_) element -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In minTaken atLeastMaxTaken) element

values.name: to1
values.comment:
Access its only value

    ArraySized.l1 "hi" |> ArraySized.to1
    --> "hi"
values.type: ArraySized.ArraySized (N.Exactly N.N1) element -> element

values.type: ArraySized.ArraySized (N.Exactly N.N10) element -> Toop.T10 element element element element element element element element element element

values.name: toArray
values.comment:
Convert to an `Array`.
Make these kinds of conversions your final step.
Try to keep extra information as long as you can: ["wrap early, unwrap late"](https://elm-radio.com/episode/wrap-early-unwrap-late)

    import N exposing (n4)
    import Array

    ArraySized.until n4
        |> ArraySized.map N.toInt
        |> ArraySized.toArray
    --> Array.fromList [ 0, 1, 2, 3, 4 ]
values.type: ArraySized.ArraySized length_ element -> Array.Array element

values.name: toChunksOf
values.comment:
Split the `ArraySized` into equal-sized (except `remainder`) slices
in a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/)

  - `groups`: the ArraySized divided into equal-sized Arrs
  - `less`: values to one side that don't fill a whole group

↓

    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))
    import N exposing (n0, n5)

    ArraySized.l7 1 2 3 4 5 6 7
        |> ArraySized.toChunksOf n5 { remainder = Up }
        --: { chunks :
        --:     ArraySized (In N0 (Add7 a_))
        --:         (ArraySized (In N5 (Add5 b_)) number_)
        --: remainder :
        --:     ArraySized (In N0 (Add5 c_)) number_
        --: }
        |> .remainder
        |> ArraySized.toList
    --> [ 6, 7 ]


    ArraySized.l7 1 2 3 4 5 6 7
        |> ArraySized.toChunksOf n5 { remainder = Down }
        |> .remainder
        |> ArraySized.toList
    --> [ 1, 2 ]
values.type: N.N (N.In (N.Add1 chunkSizeMinMinus1) (N.Add1 chunkSizeMaxMinus1) chunkSizeDifference_) -> { remainder : Linear.DirectionLinear } -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In minLength_ max) element -> { chunks : ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In N.N0 max) (ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In (N.Add1 chunkSizeMinMinus1) (N.Add1 chunkSizeMaxMinus1)) element), remainder : ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In N.N0 chunkSizeMaxMinus1) element }

values.name: toEmptiable
values.comment:
On [`empty`](#empty) `Nothing`, on [`l1`](#l1) `Just` it's only value.

Sadly, they way natural number constraints are defined,
emptiness type information can't be transferred.
values.type: ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In min_ N.N1) element -> Emptiable.Emptiable element Possibly.Possibly

values.name: toList
values.comment:
Convert to a `List`.
Make these kinds of conversions your final step.
Try to keep extra information as long as you can: ["wrap early, unwrap late"](https://elm-radio.com/episode/wrap-early-unwrap-late)

    import N exposing (n4)

    ArraySized.until n4
        |> ArraySized.map N.toInt
        |> ArraySized.toList
    --> [ 0, 1, 2, 3, 4 ]
values.type: ArraySized.ArraySized length_ element -> List.List element

values.name: until
values.comment:
Increasing natural numbers until including a given number.

    import N exposing (n3)

    ArraySized.until n3
    --: ArraySized
    --:     (In N4 (Add4 a))
    --:     (N (N.In N0 (Add3 a)))
        |> ArraySized.map N.toInt
        |> ArraySized.toList
    --> [ 0, 1, 2, 3 ]

    ArraySized.until between2And9
        |> ArraySized.map (N.add n3)
    --: ArraySized
    --:    (In N3 (Add10 a))
    --:    (N (N.In N3 (Add12 a)))
values.type: N.N (N.In min max difference_) -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.In (N.Add1 min) (N.Add1 max)) (N.N (N.In N.N0 max {}))
